steps.title: Identify corresponding angles from similarity
steps.content: Since triangle $ABC$ is similar to triangle $DEF$ and angle $A$ corresponds to angle $D$, these angles are equal: $\angle A = \angle D$. Therefore, $\sin D = \sin A$.

steps.title: Calculate sin D from tan A using the Pythagorean relationship
steps.content:
In right triangle $ABC$ (with right angle at $C$), we have $\tan A = \frac{21}{20}$, meaning the opposite side $BC = 21$ and adjacent side $AC = 20$ (in the same units). Using the Pythagorean theorem:
$$AB = \sqrt{21^2 + 20^2} = \sqrt{441 + 400} = \sqrt{841} = 29$$
Therefore:
$$\sin D = \sin A = \frac{BC}{AB} = \frac{21}{29} \approx 0.7241$$
